flywheel.page_desc: SQL query history + keyword annotations form an optimization loop for tokenization few-shots and keyword recall.

history.page_desc: Inspect every Agent turn — steps, tool calls, LLM messages, and cumulative latency.

history.select_hint_title: ← Select a thread to see details
history.select_hint_desc: Expand each step to see tool calls and LLM messages

knowledge.page_desc: Ol — business rules, calibrations, and filter hints distilled by the Lakehouse Agent from conversations. Confirmed entries are auto-applied on next query.
knowledge.empty_hint_default: Use the propose_learned_fact tool in Lakehouse Agent chat to generate facts

token_recall.page_desc: Token → keyword_explanation (3-tier) → mapped_table.field → ont_property → Od → Ok full path.
token_recall.question_placeholder: Example: X11-gen product orders by week, brand distribution
token_recall.recall_error_no_token: Please enter at least one token
token_recall.result_ambiguity_desc: The following filter values hit multiple Ods but the result lacks a dimension anchor (root "one" side missing from recall). The Agent will trigger clarify_and_branch to create a clarification sub-thread.
token_recall.result_no_ambiguity_desc: All Ods connect via dimension anchor (root dominator); query can run directly
token_recall.result_vec_note: (debug view only; not visible to LLM; threshold ≥0.85 to actually correct)
token_recall.result_vec_unavailable: Vector candidates unavailable: embedding service down or no vectors in this project
token_recall.result_direct_od_subtitle: name / display name / ont_alias direct match
token_recall.result_context_subtitle: Content injected into the system prompt

objects.import_mode_merge: MERGE: incremental upsert by natural key
objects.import_mode_skip: SKIP: insert only entries that don't exist

objects.bulk_edit_mode_append_hint: Append the text below to existing content (new line)

objects.bulk_delete_orphan_desc: Items below have no FK constraint and will not be cascaded; they will become orphaned records:

detail.alias_empty: No aliases yet — once added, token-recall will anchor matching tokens directly to this Od

intent.csv_row_objectid_empty: Row {row}: objectId / objectName cannot be empty
intent.csv_min_rows: CSV needs at least a header + 1 data row
intent.csv_missing_name_col: CSV header must include the name column
intent.csv_missing_object_col: CSV header must include objectName or objectId column
intent.csv_missing_metric_col: CSV header must include the canonicalMetric column
intent.help_aria: What is a 口径 (Metric)? (quick read)
intent.help_subtitle: A 口径 is the minimal measure definition on an OD. It doesn't know your specific question, yet one 口径 answers many. 3-minute read: boundaries, what to put in / leave out, and the three property states.
intent.bulk_create_title: Paste CSV / JSON to bulk create metrics
intent.batch_delete_title: Delete selected metrics (with cascading trigger words)
intent.bulk_edit_mark_hint: All selected metrics' active status will be set to {value}
intent.bulk_edit_priority_hint: All selected metrics' priority will be set to {value}
intent.bulk_edit_object_hint: All selected metrics will be rebound to this Od
intent.bulk_delete_warning: This action cannot be undone. The related data listed below will be deleted along with the metrics.
intent.csv_object_note: Note: objectName is auto-resolved against project Ods; only basic fields are supported. Configure filters / pivot per row after creation.

new.page_subtitle: Template: when an owning Od is detected, auto-inject measure / groupBy / filter and reshape results per pivot config

edit.delete_confirm: Delete this metric? This cannot be undone.

metric.error_no_triggers: Trigger keywords cannot be empty — add at least 1 before submitting.

new.error_no_triggers: Trigger keywords cannot be empty — add at least 1 before submitting.

edit.delete_confirm: Delete metric "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
edit.not_found: Metric does not exist or has been deleted

editor.f_mark_hint: — when off, recall skips this metric
editor.triggers_empty_warn: Triggers empty — this metric can't be recalled; submit will be rejected
editor.p_description_ph: Which order type would you like to query?
editor.filters_empty: No filters — group only by auto_group_by
editor.response_template_ph: {total} pcs total, {real} converted to Real Order
editor.generated_sql_hint: Click Run to generate and execute SQL from the parameter sampleValues.
editor.run_hint: Click Run to execute and preview results using the parameter sampleValues.
editor.run_missing_required: Required parameter(s) missing a preview value: {names}. Fill in their SAMPLE VALUE in the left panel, then run.
editor.v_od: Please select at least 1 owning OD
editor.v_query_sql: Please enter SQL (required in SQL mode)
editor.v_triggers: Please add at least 1 trigger keyword
editor.ods_hint: * at least 1; may span multiple ODs (SQL JOIN)
editor.ods_empty_warn: No OD selected — tick at least 1 OD
editor.ods_primary_hint: Primary OD (odIds[0]) — structured mode queries against it
editor.od_browser_selected_hint: — shows properties of selected ODs only · hover a property to insert a param token
editor.od_browser_empty_selected: No OD selected — tick ODs on the left first
editor.insert_column_hint: Click to insert column ref "PropName" into SQL
editor.params_derived_hint: Auto-parsed from the SQL’s {sys.req/opt.X} tokens (read-only) · write them in the SQL
editor.params_empty_derived: No parameters — write {sys.req.COLUMN} (required) or {sys.opt.COLUMN} (optional) in the SQL
editor.insert_filter_hint: Insert an optional filter predicate "PropName" = {sys.opt.PropName} (pruned at runtime when no value is given)
editor.insert_list_hint: Insert an optional multi-value filter "PropName" = ANY({sys.opt.PropName}) (SAMPLE VALUE accepts comma-separated values)
editor.rendered_sql_hint: — live-rendered for the current SAMPLE VALUEs (absent optionals pruned, lists bound)
editor.rendered_sql_empty: Write SQL above to see the actual pruned query here, live.
editor.rendered_missing: Missing required parameter(s) (need a SAMPLE VALUE): {names}
editor.tm_legend: Test mode (affects Run / rendered SQL): use = send the SAMPLE VALUE; omit = key absent from payload; null = key present but empty value (scalar '', list [])
editor.tm_omit_hint: The key is omitted from the payload entirely — simulates the LLM not mentioning the param at all. Engine takes the prune path.
editor.tm_null_hint: The key is present in the payload but with an empty value (scalar → "", list → []). Simulates the LLM mentioning the param but having no value. Engine currently behaves identically to 'omit' — useful to confirm equivalence.
editor.tm_omit_placeholder: (omit · current test mode omits the key from payload)
editor.tm_null_placeholder: (null · current test mode sends an empty "" / [])
editor.metric_sql_hint: — describes the metric itself: single OD, one aggregate, no JOIN/filter/token
editor.metric_sql_note: On save the backend parses this SQL → primary OD, measure (sum/avg/...), base dims. Runtime composition (extra dims, cross-OD JOIN, filters) is driven by the function-call JSON.
editor.metric_sql_browser_no_od: Pick a primary OD on the left to see its columns here
editor.metric_sql_browser_no_cols: This OD has no properties yet
editor.metric_sql_browser_od_title: Click to insert the OD name (use in FROM)
editor.metric_sql_browser_col_title: Click to insert this column into the SQL
editor.preview_hint: (run the SQL above as a plain query, see the result)
editor.preview_note: No runtime filters/grouping applied — just runs the SQL you wrote. For complex what-ifs use the simulator.
editor.simulator_pointer: Test runtime behavior (filters / cross-OD JOIN / ordering / dimensions) in the Function-Call Simulator — use the 'Open simulator' button in the top bar (save the metric first).
editor.simulator_hint: — paste a function-call JSON; see the SQL the engine actually assembles + the result (same path the agent runtime uses)
editor.sim_filters: Filters (as the LLM would pass at runtime)
editor.sim_col_ph: column: bare for this OD, or OD.Column for cross-OD
editor.sim_val_ph: value (empty = show dimension only, no filter)
editor.sim_filter_note: Value set = filter + show as dimension; empty = break down by it only; an OD.Column reference auto-joins that OD.
editor.legacy_sql_banner: This is a legacy SQL-mode metric (with {sys.req/opt.NAME} tokens). The new model uses a single-layer bare SQL + JSON-driven runtime composition. The row is preserved; migrate when convenient.

simulator.props_hint: pick an OD column → setting a value makes it a filter, leaving it empty makes it a dimension
simulator.props_note: Value set = filter + show as dimension; empty = break down by it only; picking another OD's column auto-joins it.
simulator.json_hint: — same config as the form on the left; this is the real payload
simulator.sql_empty: Click Simulate to see the SQL the engine assembles.
simulator.result_empty: Click Simulate to see the result.

keyword.bulk_create_title: Paste CSV / JSON to bulk create
keyword.bulk_reanchor_title: Re-anchor to another property / metric / clear anchor

keyword.csv_required_hint: Required: keyword · plus either od+property or propertyId
keyword.alias_equiv_title: Alias (treated as equivalent to {keyword} at recall)

passthrough.title: Ontology SQL Pass-through
passthrough.subtitle: Write SELECT statements using Od names as tables and Property names as columns. The server injects canonical_query as a CTE and pushes down to the physical tables.
passthrough.pill_readonly_tip: Backend uses transaction_read_only = on; any INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE / DDL is rejected
passthrough.pill_timeout_tip: statement_timeout = 30s; on timeout Postgres aborts and rolls back the transaction
passthrough.pill_whitelist_tip: FROM / JOIN can only reference Ontology objects with canonical_query configured in this project; DDL keywords are blocked before frontend validation
passthrough.schema_empty: No Ods configured in this project
passthrough.schema_empty_hint: Solidify canonical_query on the Lakehouse Objects page first
passthrough.od_not_queryable: This Od has no canonical_query and cannot be queried directly
passthrough.mc_title: machine code column (use the canonical property name)
passthrough.snippets_empty_hint: Click "Save" above to save the current SQL
passthrough.editor_hint: chars · select text to run partial query
passthrough.idle_hint: Press ⌘↵ or click Run to execute
passthrough.idle_sub: Tables = Od names · Columns = Property names · up to {rowLimit} rows
passthrough.avail_od_hint: Available Ods (click to insert into editor):
passthrough.empty_result_hint: Query syntax is valid, but no rows matched

mcp.page_subtitle: Generate access credentials for external tools (Claude Desktop, Cursor)
mcp.security_1: The key is shown in full only at creation; you cannot view it again
mcp.security_2: Do not commit the key to a repo or share it
mcp.security_3: If the key is leaked, revoke it immediately on this page
mcp.docs_intro: Add the following to your Claude Desktop or Cursor MCP config file:
mcp.docs_note_body: Replace YOUR_API_KEY above with the actual key value
mcp.empty_hint: Create a key to connect external tools via the MCP protocol
mcp.tool_lookup_hint: Fetch object definition + property list by name
mcp.tool_smartquery_hint: Run Postgres SQL from a QuerySpec
mcp.label_placeholder: e.g. Claude Desktop on my Mac
mcp.new_key_warning: Copy and save it now — you won't be able to see the full key again
mcp.auth_code_example:
# HTTP header — either works:
Authorization: Bearer mcp_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
# or
X-API-Key: mcp_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
mcp.auth_note: The key is shown in full only once at creation — store it safely
mcp.claude_config_paste_hint: Paste the following JSON into Claude Desktop's mcpServers config
mcp.conn_docs_hint: Copy the endpoint and key below into your MCP client
mcp.key_one_time_warning: This key is shown only once — copy and store it now
mcp.label_hint: For your own identification (not exposed externally)
mcp.no_keys_hint: Create a key to connect external tools via MCP
mcp.restrict_tools_hint: When checked, this key can only call the selected tools
mcp.revoke_confirm: Revoke this key? It will be invalidated immediately.
mcp.security_4: If the key leaks, revoke it on this page immediately
mcp.security_5: Use a separate key per client
